reels.caption: 🇺🇸 Real fights still exist. But now it feels like every big UFC card also needs chaos, fake beef, viral moments and dram
good: Hook uses 'FAKE' in bold red over UFC action—high-contrast scroll-stop with controversy angle that drives 25.5% engagement and 5.5% share rate
Narrative arc (feels fake → UFC/WWE merger context → viewership decline proof) maintains watch-through with credible structure
Bilingual execution (English + Portuguese subtitles) naturally expands reach without friction; speaks directly to your audience split
Strong share rate (127 shares) shows opinion-driven content spreads; audience actively reposts this take
bad: 3-second mark: static talking head against plain background kills momentum right after powerful hook—Reels require constant visual energy
End-frame viewership numbers (9,204 → 2,904) are too small to read; visual weight doesn't land with same impact as dramatic hook
Missing explicit CTA—no 'comment your take', 'drop your thoughts', or 'share if you agree' prompt leaves engagement conversion on table
Implicit link between 'fake fights' and viewership drop could be one beat clearer; why exactly did numbers decline?
reels.top_tip: Replace static talking-head at 3s with dynamic B-roll, lower-thirds graphics, or fight clips to preserve post-hook momentum. Add bold text CTA at end: 'Comment: Real fights or WWE theatre?' to convert engagement into comments and saves.

reels.caption: 🇧🇷 O Daukhaus parece mais limpo na trocação, mas o problema é sobreviver às entradas constantes do Bo Nickal. Se encosta
good: Exceptional engagement rate (85.9%) and watch time (29.6s avg) prove content hooks viewers immediately after they start watching
Strong shareability signal: 126 shares indicate prediction/debate format resonates with fight community
Expert credibility and analysis format builds trust and holding power through the reel
Multi-language captions (PT/EN) expand addressable audience
bad: Low absolute view count (1,474) despite high engagement—hook isn't stopping enough initial scrollers in the feed
Only 4 saves—content reads as entertainment, not reference material; missing save-triggering elements (stats, strategy breakdown, odds)
Text overlay contrast in mid-frames could be sharper for mobile viewing (black text on dark backgrounds risks legibility)
reels.top_tip: Increase hook aggressiveness in first 1 second: make the prediction question larger/bolder and lead with the most visually dramatic action clip to stop scrollers before they move on—your engagement metrics show the content works once watched, so unlocking reach is the bottleneck

reels.caption: 🇧🇷 Bonfim tem talento pra virar um dos maiores problemas da divisão. Mas o Belal transforma qualquer luta em pressão e d
good: Dominant hook: shirtless fighter close-up immediately stops scroll with visual impact
Strong narrative framing: fighter comparison creates intrigue and narrative tension
High engagement rate (38%) and share count (138) shows viral/entertainment appeal
Effective engagement trigger: direct question ('right?') mid-video prompts comments
Bilingual Portuguese/English caption captures wider geographic audience
bad: Extremely low save rate (4 saves on 2,171 views) signals content feels entertainment-only, not valuable enough to keep
Reach is only 64% of views (1,390/2,171)—algorithm isn't amplifying; limited potential detected
Branding outro card is too long (~4 seconds), taking up 25% of reel without value—viewers exit during credits instead of following
No verbal/on-screen follow CTA until final card—misses conversion window when engagement is highest (5-10s mark)
reels.top_tip: Cut branding outro to 2 seconds maximum; add visible 'Follow for fight breakdowns' CTA at 5-second mark to convert engaged viewers before they scroll away—currently losing subscriber gains to logo screen

reels.caption: 🇧🇷 Diego é muito perigoso quando consegue encurtar e misturar pressão com grappling. Mas no centro, a velocidade do Garc
good: Exceptional 60.1% engagement rate proves strong audience resonance with technical analysis
Dramatic hook with immediate action and Peace Grappler branding—stops scrolls effectively
Bilingual captions (Portuguese/English) expand reach without alienating either market
Expert talking-head commentary with branded merch builds creator credibility over raw fight clips
bad: Zero shares despite high engagement—content entertains but lacks discussion/debate value
Only 1 save rate—viewers aren't bookmarking technical grappling reference despite focus on technique
Talking head segments (frames 2-3) interrupt action pacing—fight footage drives engagement, commentary slows it
Missing shareability hook—no prediction, bold take, or CTA to convert engagement into viral spread
reels.top_tip: Add a prediction or controversial take ('Garcia's speed fades in transitions—Lopes wins if he X' + 'tag someone who agrees') to convert engagement into shares; combat sports audiences thrive on debate, not passive appreciation

reels.caption: 🇺🇸Very few fighters reach the top. Even fewer do it by knocking out names like Volkanovski, Holloway and Oliveira. These
good: Immediate action hook: UFC fight fills frame with bold "TOPURIA TOP 5 KNOCKOUTS" text—stops scroll effectively
Strong average watch time (8.9s): Viewers stay through most of the video, indicating good pacing and flow
High engagement rate (70.4%): Direct CTA ("which one was your favorite?") successfully drives comment interaction
Clear countdown format with fighter names: Easy to follow and maintains momentum through the video
bad: Critically low save rate (only 1 save from 664 views): Content isn't sticky enough to bookmark—no perceived value for reference
Poor share performance (9 shares = 1.3%): Generic Top 5 list lacks novelty; viewers don't feel compelled to send to others
Anticlimactic ending with no payoff: Countdown finishes without reveal, surprise, or reason to engage further
reels.top_tip: Replace the generic countdown with a narrative ending: reveal #1 with a shocking stat, create a voting mechanic for a follow-up reel, or end with a controversial hot take—engagement comments prove interest, but the ending needs to make people save and share

reels.caption: 🇧🇷 Gabriel Bonfim, Joanderson Brito, Bruno Silva, Ketlen Souza, Ariane Carnelossi, Priscila Cachoeira e mais brasileiros
good: Strong hook: fighter in action stance with professional branding creates immediate visual interest
Clear fighter identification: text overlays with names and event details build audience familiarity
High engagement rate (51.6%): content resonates with fight sports audience despite moderate reach
Consistent visual identity: Peace Grappler branding and UFC logos add production credibility
bad: Low shares (5) and saves (1): despite action-focused content, lacks shareability hook or clear reason to save
Weak call-to-action: no visible engagement prompt (e.g., 'Who you got?', polls, or prediction asks) in frames
Moderate views (815) relative to reach (517): suggests hook may not be stopping enough passive scrollers
Hook could be more dramatic: stance shot is solid but lacks the knockout/submission/big strike moment that drives viral action
reels.top_tip: Replace the hook with the most intense action moment (submission, knockdown, or striking exchange) and add an overlay text question like 'Who takes it?' to drive saves, shares, and comments

reels.caption: 🇺🇸 Gabriel Bonfim moves from #11 to the UFC Welterweight Top 5 after a dominant decision win over Belal Muhammad. The ca
good: Strong visual hook with immediate fighter contact and UFC branding creates instant recognition
High engagement rate (56.7%) shows viewers who see it stay engaged and interact
Clear action in early frames — submission/ground control is visually dramatic and holds attention
Professional overlays and text readability maintain production quality throughout
bad: View count (441) is severely underperforming — weak initial distribution suggests algorithmic suppression
Share rate (1) is critically low — caption tries to cover multiple fighters/stats and dilutes the story
Caption lacks clear narrative focus and CTA — viewers don't know what to take away or why to share
Dense text overlay in hook may compete with the visual action instead of amplifying it
reels.top_tip: Reframe caption to focus exclusively on Bonfim's breakthrough moment (minor to top 5) with a single clear stat, then add a question CTA like 'Who's next for him?' — currently covering too many fighters splits the narrative and tanks shareability
